marketState: neutral, mild bullish tilt — extreme heat-driven demand and stretched short positioning offset by a comfortable storage surplus
summary: Price ($2.911) is sitting almost exactly on fair value at every horizon (spreads of just -1c to -14c, all flagged FAIR), so there is no valuation edge on the spot itself. The strongest signal in the data is weather: 8-16 day demand is +30.5% vs the 5-year norm and actually exceeds the historical max range, while 1-7 day demand is a more modest +9.5%. That sits alongside a managed-money COT position that is near a 5-year-low percentile (8th) on net shorts, raising short-covering risk if hot weather verifies. Working against that is a clearly bearish storage picture (+180 Bcf vs 5y, and this week's build beat forecast by 22 Bcf) and weak, low-reliability seasonality (price already -7.7pp below its typical-year path, with only a 38-44% historical odds of being higher in 1/3/4 weeks). Technicals are split by timeframe (15m/1h leaning constructive, 4h/1d/1w bearish), and the futures curve shows normal seasonal contango into winter, so there is no clean technical consensus to lean on for a quick trade.

longTerm.riskReward: ~1:0.9 to TP1, ~1:2.4 to TP2 (from mid-entry $2.915, stop $2.75)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; trim ~1/3 at TP1, trail stop to breakeven on remainder
longTerm.rationale: The fair-value curve itself is rising over the next three weeks ($2.923 -> $2.941 -> $2.985 -> $3.050), and 8-16 day demand is running above its historical max range (+30.5% vs 5y) — both point to firming physical tightness into peak summer cooling demand. Managed money is positioned near a 5-year-low short percentile (8th), which adds asymmetric short-covering upside if the heat verifies. The counterweight is a +180 Bcf storage surplus vs 5y and weak/low-reliability seasonality (price already -7.7pp below its typical seasonal path), which is why conviction is medium rather than high and the stop sits below near-term structure.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D weather demand forecast rolls over back toward/below the 5y norm, storage surprises keep printing bearish (further widening the +180 Bcf surplus), or price closes below ~$2.75 confirming the 1d/1w bearish technical trend is in control.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is trading essentially AT fair value (spread only -$0.012, status FAIR) so there is no near-term mispricing edge, and technicals disagree by timeframe — 15m/1h lean neutral-to-bullish while 4h/1d/1w are bearish with EMA50<200. Near-term (1-7D) weather demand is only moderately elevated (+9.5% vs 5y, rangePos 0.64), not the extreme readout seen further out, and the futures curve's slight front-month backwardation (-$0.035 to next) is too small to lean on, so it doesn't justify forcing a trade against mixed technicals.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either: (a) price breaking below ~$2.75 with 4h/1d/1w trend staying aligned bearish, confirming downside momentum for a short, or (b) 1h/4h technicals turning consistently bullish alongside a push through ~$3.00, confirming the weather-driven long thesis is being realized near-term.

keyDrivers: 8-16D weather demand +30.5% vs 5y and above the historical max range — strongest single signal in the data
Managed-money net short near 5-year-low percentile (8th) — short-covering risk if heat verifies
Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf build-vs-forecast surprise — caps upside
Price is essentially at fair value at every horizon (front spreads -1c to -14c, all FAIR) — no valuation edge either way
Seasonality reliability is Low (59pp 10-90% spread) and price already sits -7.7pp below its typical seasonal path
risks: 8-16 day weather forecasts are inherently uncertain and can revise cooler, removing the main bullish driver
Storage surplus could keep widening if production stays elevated, pressuring price independent of weather
Technicals conflict across timeframes (constructive short-term vs bearish 4h/1d/1w), raising whipsaw risk for any near-term trade
